title: A micromagnetic model with bidirectional magneto-thermal coupling
authors: Peiru Yi, Zian Xia, Weichao Yu
abstract: Most conventional micromagnetic frameworks in spin caloritronics rely on a unidirectional coupling approximation, wherein thermal fluctuations drive magnetization dynamics while the feedback of magnetic dissipation onto the thermal reservoir is neglected. Here, we establish a rigorously self-consistent bidirectional magneto-thermal coupling model by integrating the stochastic Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (sLLG) equation with a generalized heat transfer equation. In this closed-loop framework, the local temperature acts as a dynamical variable, and the damping-induced dissipation alongside stochastic work dynamically feeds back into the thermal bath as localized heat sources. Utilizing Ito stochastic calculus, we analytically prove that this coupled system strictly obeys the first law of thermodynamics and spontaneously recovers the correct Boltzmann statistics at equilibrium. Spatially resolved micromagnetic simulations further validate the energy exchange mechanism, capturing the finite-bath temperature reduction induced by spatial variation of magnetic moments and the modified density of states under exchange interactions. This bidirectional framework provides a robust microscopic foundation for investigating complex nonequilibrium magneto-thermal dynamics, such as the unidirectional spin-wave heat conveyer effect, paving the way for advanced spin-caloritronic applications.
summary: This paper develops a self-consistent model coupling stochastic magnetization dynamics with thermal heat transfer. By treating energy dissipation as a feedback source into the thermal bath, it rigorously proves thermodynamic consistency and predicts measurable temperature changes. This advances spin-caloritronics by providing a robust framework for non-equilibrium magneto-thermal studies.
main_problem: To overcome the limitations of conventional micromagnetic models that use unidirectional coupling, this work establishes a rigorously self-consistent bidirectional magneto-thermal coupling framework.
main_result: The coupled system is shown to strictly obey the first law of thermodynamics and spontaneously recovers correct Boltzmann statistics at equilibrium. The model predicts measurable temperature drops ($\Delta T$) in the thermal bath due to magnetic dynamics.
method: The authors integrate the stochastic Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (sLLG) equation with a generalized heat transfer equation, utilizing Ito stochastic calculus for rigorous energy balance analysis.
model_or_system: The system models coupled spin dynamics and thermal evolution in a magnetic material. The core components are the sLLG equation governing magnetization ($\mathbf{m}$) and a heat diffusion equation governing local temperature ($T$).
key_observables: Local temperature field $T(\mathbf{r}, t)$, energy exchange mechanism (dissipation vs. work), density of states $g(E)$, and the steady-state temperature reduction $\Delta T$.
important_parameters: Damping coefficient ($\alpha$), thermal conductivity ($\kappa_{	ext{th}}$), initial temperature ($T_0$), and system volume ($\Delta V$).
main_assumptions: The analysis often relies on thermodynamic adiabatic approximations, small angle expansions, and the assumption that local energy balance can be closed into a self-consistent loop.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the bidirectional energy exchange (dissipation feeding heat), the time evolution of bath temperature towards steady state, and histograms confirming recovery of Boltzmann statistics.
paper_structure: The paper develops the coupled equations by deriving local power balances, applies Ito calculus to ensure thermodynamic consistency, validates equilibrium recovery via statistical checks, and finally simulates macroscopic energy exchange effects like $\Delta T$.
why_interesting: This work provides a detailed microscopic foundation for non-equilibrium thermodynamics in magnetic systems, which is crucial for understanding heat transport phenomena relevant to quantum devices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic illustration of the bidirectional magneto- thermal energy exchange mechanism. The magnetic subsys- tem dissipates energy into the thermal reservoir via Gilbert damping, whereas the thermal reservoir reciprocally transfers energy back to the magnetic system through the work per- formed by stochastic thermal fields.

Figure 2. Time evolution of the heat bath temperature under bidirectional coupling. (a) Single-point temperature trajec- tory simulated using Ito calculation with gyromagnetic ra- tio γ = 2.21 × 103 m/(A · s) , macrospin volume of V = 5 × 10−22 m3 and initial temperature T0 = 2 K. In micro- magnetic simulation, one time step equals 1 × 10−2ns. (b) Spatially averaged temperature evolution over 200 micromag- netic cells simulated in COMSOL Multiphysics with cell vol- ume ∆V = 5×10−22 m3. The blue shaded region indicates the 1σ confidence interval of the temperature distribution across spatial points. Inset: Steady-state temperature variance as a function of various initial temperatures T0.

Figure 3. Verification of the Boltzmann statistical distribution using COMSOL Multiphysics. The 1D system consists of 200 cells with volume ∆V = 5×10−22 m3, time step ∆t = 1 ps, and damping α = 0.05. (a) Semi-logarithmic plot of the numerically sampled ln(P/g) versus energy E, showing strict linear agreement with the theoretical slope −β. (b)-(d) Statistical histograms of the magnetic moment energy distribution at thermal equilibrium for T = 100 K, 50 K, and 10 K, respectively. The solid lines represent the analytical prediction P(E) ∝g(E)e−E/kBT .

Figure 4. Equilibrium temperature variation of the finite heat bath. (a) Temperature change ∆T versus initial temperature T0. (b) Temperature change ∆T versus the macrospin volume ∆V . The simulation parameters are ∆V = 5 × 10−22 m3, T0 = 10 K, ∆t = 1 ps, and α = 5 × 10−3.
